etymology.description: 澤(澤) (zé, "marsh") refers to a wetland or marsh, indicating a moist and fertile area. 東(東) (dōng, "east") signifies the direction east. The combination in a personal name like 毛泽东 (Máo Zédōng) suggests renown or auspiciousness associated with the east.
etymology.interpretation: In personal names, 澤 (zé, "marsh") conveys a sense of growth or abundance, as marshes are fertile. 東 (dōng, "east") is often culturally seen as an auspicious direction, symbolizing new beginnings or drawn associations with the rising sun. Thus, 澤東 (Zédōng) in names can imply thriving prosperity from the east.

examples.chinese: 毛泽东(毛澤東)是中国历史上的一个重要人物(中國歷史上的一個重要人物)。
examples.pinyin: Máo Zédōng shì Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng de yī gè zhòngyào rénwù.
examples.english: Mao Zedong is an important figure in Chinese history.

examples.chinese: 泽东(澤東)河畔的风景非常美丽(河畔的風景非常美麗)。
examples.pinyin: Zédōng hépàn de fēngjǐng fēicháng měilì.
examples.english: The scenery by the eastern marsh is very beautiful.

examples.chinese: 他在阅读毛泽东(毛澤東)的著作(的著作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yuèdú Máo Zédōng de zhùzuò.
examples.english: He is reading the works of Mao Zedong.
